title: cracca anthylloides
intro: Cracca anthylloides, also known as Kidney Vetch, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Cracca anthylloides can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and relieve various health complaints.
